macro_segments.title: Review of Previous Discussion: Raw vs. Roasted Korban Pesach and the Prohibition on Eating Before Erev Pesach

micro_segments.title: The meaning of 'na' (raw/undercooked) versus roasted meat in the Korban Pesach
Whether eating the Korban Pesach before Erev Pesach transgresses a positive or negative mitzvah

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's Opinion on Negative Prohibitions Implied by Positive Mitzvot

micro_segments.title: The baraita about blemished animals: can they be sanctified as korban or only donated for proceeds
The drasha on 'vayedaber Hashem el Moshe lemor' to derive a negative prohibition from a positive one
Discussion of whether the drasha applies universally or only in specific contexts

macro_segments.title: Water Used by Bakers and the Requirement to Spill It Out

micro_segments.title: The requirement to spill baker's water on an incline vs. level ground to prevent chametz formation
Rav Yehuda's requirement for mayim shelannu (water that slept overnight) and the pun about 'shelannu'

macro_segments.title: Rava's Stringent Requirements for Women Kneading Dough on Pesach

micro_segments.title: Restrictions on using hot water, sun-heated water, or water from heated pots; constant kneading requirement
The need for two vessels: one for working dough and one for cooling hands
Debate between Mar Zutra and Rav Ashi regarding post-facto permissibility of using warm water

macro_segments.title: Introduction to Third Perek: Non-Chametz Foods Prohibited on Pesach

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah lists items like Babylonian kutach, Median beer, Edomian vinegar, Egyptian zeisom, dyers' plaster, butchers' dough, and scribes' glue
Rabbi Eliezer's principle: anything with grain component transgresses; distinction between transgression with and without karet

macro_segments.title: Egyptian Zeisom and Other Professional/Non-Food Items Containing Grain

micro_segments.title: Discussion of why these items are prohibited despite being mixtures or non-foods: mixture (ta'aruvet), incomplete fermentation (chametz nuksha), or lack of edibility (ra'ui l'kelev)

macro_segments.title: Analysis of Babylonian Kutach and Historical Context of Wine Quality

micro_segments.title: Description of Babylonian kutach's three harmful effects: heart blockage, eye blindness, and body weakness
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's teaching on the change in wine quality before and after the Churban Habayis
Talmudic discussions on vinegar made from temet (grape lees water) and the requirement to separate terumah and ma'aser

macro_segments.title: Egyptian Zeisom and Other Professional/Non-Food Items Containing Grain (continued)

micro_segments.title: Composition of Egyptian zeisom as one-third barley, one-third saffron, one-third salt; its use as a laxative
Dyers' plaster (zoman) used in leather work and scribes' glue (kolan) used by leatherworkers and for gluing papers
Butchers' dough placed on pot lips to absorb froth, possibly not fully fermented or not fit for human consumption

topical_tags.term: Rabbi Yehuda (opinion on negative prohibitions from positive mitzvot)
Vayedaber Hashem el Moshe Lemor (drasha on negative implications)
Terumah and Ma'aser (priestly gifts and tithes)
Rav Yehuda (baker's water and mayim shelannu)
Mar Zutra vs. Rav Ashi (warm water b'diavad)
Bal Yira'eh u'Bal Yimatzeh (biblical prohibition against owning/seeing chametz)
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak (wine quality teaching)
